1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12. 
13.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>
25.

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

Both custom targets in `IncrementalBroken.csproj` will **always re-run** and **force recompilation** on every build:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** — MSBuild can never skip this target.
- **Volatile property `DateTime::Now`** — The file content changes every build, so even if you added Inputs/Outputs, the written file would differ each time, invalidating CoreCompile's inputs and triggering a full recompile.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** — MSBuild can never skip this target.
- **Unconditional `Overwrite="true"`** — Even when the git hash hasn't changed (no new commit), the file is rewritten, updating its timestamp. This makes CoreCompile see a "changed" input and recompile unnecessarily.

### Net Effect
Every `dotnet build` triggers both targets → both write files into `obj/` → CoreCompile sees changed inputs → **full recompilation every time**, even when no source code changed.

### Recommended Fixes
| Target | Fix |
|--------|-----|
| `GenerateTimestamp` | Remove it or gate it behind a CI-only condition (`Condition="'$(CI)'=='true'"`). Timestamps are inherently anti-incremental. |
| `EmbedGitHash` | Add `Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects)" Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"` and use a **hash-compare** write (only overwrite when content differs) to avoid touching the file timestamp unnecessarily. |
data.reasoningText:
Now I can analyze the incremental build issues. Let me identify the problems:

1. **`GenerateTimestamp` target** (line 6-11):
   - **No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes** — MSBuild cannot determine if this target is up-to-date, so it runs every time.
   - **Uses `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())`** — This is a volatile property that changes on every build invocation. Even if Inputs/Outputs were added, the content written to `build-timestamp.txt` would differ every time, causing downstream targets (like CoreCompile) to detect changed inputs and re-execute.
   - The file gets written unconditionally each build, breaking incremental compilation.

2. **`EmbedGitHash` target** (line 13-23):
   - **No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes** — MSBuild can't skip this target either.
   - The git hash itself is stable between builds unless a new commit happens, but without declaring inputs and outputs, the target always re-runs and unconditionally overwrites `git-hash.g.cs`.
   - Even when the file content hasn't changed, updating its timestamp causes CoreCompile to think its inputs were modified, which defeats incremental builds. The `<Compile Include>` for the generated file is fine, but the unconditional rewrite is the real problem.
